title: Image Deblurring (non-blind and blind deconvolution)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in classical deconvolution literature (Richardson 1972; Lucy 1974; Krishnan-Fergus 2009 blind deconvolution; Pan et al. 2016 dark-channel deblurring).

E.description: A latent sharp image x is degraded by a shift-invariant 2D convolution kernel k (motion blur, out-of-focus blur, atmospheric turbulence) and additive sensor noise. Recovery is either non-blind (kernel known) or blind (kernel jointly estimated with image).
E.world_state_x: Sharp image x in R^{H x W} (grayscale) or R^{H x W x 3} (color), piecewise-smooth with sparse gradients
E.observation_y: Blurred noisy image y in R^{H x W x C}, same spatial grid as x
physical_parameters_theta: Blur kernel k of support K x K (K in [5, 51])
Blur type: motion (linear or curved), defocus (disk), Gaussian, atmospheric (Kolmogorov r0)
Sensor read noise sigma_n and gain
Sub-pixel shift (dx, dy) and rotation theta of kernel

W.regime: Non-blind: deconvolution is ill-conditioned with kappa proportional to |OTF|^-1 at high frequencies; Tikhonov regularization bounds kappa_eff. Blind: bilinear in (k, x) and non-convex; only locally well-posed under sparse-gradient prior.

C.solver_class: Wiener filter, Richardson-Lucy, TV-ADMM, plug-and-play (PnP-BM3D), learned (SRN-DeblurNet, MPRNet, NAFNet)
C.error_bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_n * sqrt(sum |1/OTF_k|^2) + C2 * bias(prior); blind case requires coarse-to-fine + heavy-tail prior to avoid delta-solution trap
C.complexity: O(H*W*log(H*W)) per FFT iteration; blind MAP typically 10-100 iterations; learned methods single forward pass O(H*W*C)
